platform_compatibility.title: AVIF Browser & Platform Compatibility
platform_compatibility.body: AVIF is supported in Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Edge 121+, and Safari 16.4+, covering approximately 93% of global users. On Windows 10/11, AVIF can be opened natively in Photos after installing the AV1 Video Extension from the Microsoft Store. On Mac, macOS Ventura (13) and later support AVIF natively in Preview and Quick Look. On iPhone and iPad, iOS 16+ supports AVIF in Safari and the Photos app. For users on older browsers, serve AVIF alongside a WebP or JPG fallback using the HTML 'picture' element for maximum compatibility.

beforeAfter.title: WebP vs AVIF: See the Difference
beforeAfter.description: AVIF takes compression further. Compare file size and visual quality side by side.

items.title: Upgrade Your Image Pipeline

items.title: Reduce Bandwidth & Storage Costs
items.description: Already using WebP? AVIF squeezes out even more savings. A 100 KB WebP image can drop to 80–90 KB in AVIF at identical visual quality.

items.title: HDR & Wide Color Gamut
items.description: WebP is limited to 8-bit color. AVIF supports 10-bit and 12-bit HDR content, making it ideal for high-dynamic-range photos and modern displays.

items.title: Future-Proof Your Assets
items.description: AVIF browser adoption is growing rapidly. Converting your WebP images to AVIF now ensures your assets are in the most future-proof format available.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is AVIF actually better than WebP?
faq.a1: Yes. AVIF typically achieves 10–20% smaller file sizes than WebP at the same visual quality, using the more advanced AV1 compression algorithm. AVIF also supports HDR (10-bit, 12-bit) color depth, which WebP does not.
faq.q2: Does converting WebP to AVIF preserve transparency?
faq.a2: Yes. AVIF fully supports the alpha channel. Any transparent areas in your WebP images will be perfectly preserved in the converted AVIF output.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert private images here?
faq.a3: Absolutely. Avifkit uses client-side processing — your images are converted entirely within your browser's memory and are never uploaded to any server. Your files never leave your device.
faq.q4: Will AVIF work in all browsers?
faq.a4: AVIF is supported in Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Edge 121+, and Safari 16.4+, which covers roughly 93% of global users. For older browsers, use the HTML 'picture' element to serve AVIF with a WebP or JPG fallback.
faq.q5: How much smaller will AVIF be compared to WebP?
faq.a5: AVIF files are typically 10–20% smaller than WebP at equivalent visual quality. The savings are most pronounced on photographic content and complex images with lots of detail.
faq.q6: Is there a file size limit?
faq.a6: Each file can be up to 10 MB. Since all processing happens in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device's performance — but there are no server-side restrictions.
faq.q7: Can I convert multiple WebP files at once?
faq.a7: Yes. Upload up to 30 WebP files at once for batch conversion. Once converted, download files individually or as a single ZIP archive.
faq.q8: Should I switch from WebP to AVIF on my website?
faq.a8: If your audience primarily uses modern browsers, yes. Use AVIF as the primary format and provide WebP or JPG fallbacks via the 'picture' element. You'll gain 10–20% additional bandwidth savings with no perceptible quality difference.
faq.q9: Do I need to create an account or install anything?
faq.a9: No. Avifkit runs entirely in your browser — no registration, no software installation, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Just open the page and start converting.
